papers.title: Mechanical properties of Fe–Al–M–C (MZTi, V, Nb, Ta) alloys with strengthening carbides and Laves phase
papers.paper_scope: Investigation of room- and elevated-temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture behaviour of Fe-15Al and Fe-26Al based alloys containing carbide and/or Laves phase precipitates formed by additions of C with Ti, V, Nb, or Ta.

processing_steps.method: vacuum induction melting and copper mould casting
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Cast into cylindrical copper moulds (32 mm diameter, 186 mm length). Specimens cut from cast ingots by spark erosion along the longitudinal axis. Compression samples: 6×6×10 mm³. Tensile samples: 3.5×5 mm² cross-section, 15 mm gauge length. Surfaces ground. Mechanical tests at deformation rate of 10^-4 s^-1 using ZWICK 1474.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 159 HB. Tensile yield stress 344 MPa, total elongation 16%. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC).

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 197 HB. Tensile yield stress 420 MPa, total elongation 3.3%. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC). Dendritic structure with fibrous/lamellar TiC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 191 HB. Tensile yield stress 374 MPa, total elongation 0.6%. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC). Columnar grains with plate-like VC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 309 HB. Very high compressive yield stress 827 MPa. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC). Dendritic structure with granular/lamellar NbC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 291 HB. Very high compressive yield stress 868 MPa. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC). Dendritic structure with fibrous/lamellar TaC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 260 HB. Tensile yield stress 475 MPa, total elongation 0.4%. Very coarse transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC).

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 251 HB. Tensile yield stress 342 MPa, total elongation 0.7%. Mixed intergranular decohesion and transgranular cleavage (IGD+TGC). Polygonal grains with cube-like TiC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 229 HB. Mixed intergranular decohesion and transgranular cleavage (IGD+TGC). Almost completely brittle intergranular decohesion. Polygonal grains with cube-like TiC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 286 HB. Tensile yield stress 350 MPa, total elongation 0.5%. Transgranular cleavage fracture (TGC) with uniform steps influenced by VC orientation. Columnar grains with plate-like VC. Pronounced tension-compression asymmetry (~36%).

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 275 HB. Tensile yield stress 427 MPa, total elongation 1.4%. Very fine facetted transgranular cleavage (TGC). Zig-zag grains with cube-like/script-like NbC.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 303 HB. Compressive yield stress 792 MPa. Mixed intergranular decohesion and transgranular cleavage (IGD+TGC). Strengthening by NbC and Laves phase (Fe,Al)2Nb. Polygonal grains with cube-like NbC and plate-like Laves phase.

characterization_methods.technique: Brinell hardness testing, uniaxial compression testing, uniaxial tensile testing, scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluation of room temperature and elevated temperature mechanical properties, hardness, and fracture surface analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Matrix hardness 288 HB. Mixed intergranular decohesion and transgranular cleavage (IGD+TGC). Polygonal and partially zig-zag grains with cube-like/script-like TaC.

unmapped_findings: Room temperature compressive yield strengths (MPa): Fe-15Al: 356; Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C: 485; Fe-15Al-2V-1C: 396; Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C: 827; Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C: 868; Fe-26Al: 644; Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C: 447; Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C: 454; Fe-26Al-2V-1C: 544; Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C: 601; Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C: 792; Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C: 594.

Fracture modes at room temperature (Table 2): Fe-15Al, Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-15Al-2V-1C, Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C, Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C, Fe-26Al, Fe-26Al-2V-1C, Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C show transgranular cleavage (TGC); Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C, Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C show intergranular decohesion (IGD) plus TGC.

Fig. 2: Room temperature compression stress-strain curves show parabolic work hardening for Fe-15Al-based alloys; Fe-26Al-based alloys exhibit very high strengthening with almost constant hardening rates until final parabolic stage. Binary Fe-26Al shows linear elastic followed by parabolic hardening.

Fig. 3: Room temperature tensile stress-strain curves indicate very poor ductility for most alloys; highest total elongation apart from binary Fe-15Al obtained for Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C (~3.3%) and Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C (~1.4%). Some alloys crack during elastic deformation.

Tension-compression asymmetry: All investigated alloys exhibit higher yield stress in compression than in tension at room temperature. Fe-26Al-2V-1C shows pronounced tension-compression asymmetry (~36%) attributed to thin sharp plate-like VC precipitates causing notch sensitivity. Possible contributions include nonplanar screw dislocation cores, Bauschinger effect from internal tensions during solidification, and precipitate notch sensitivity.

Fig. 5 temperature-dependent compressive yield stress for Fe-15Al-based alloys (MPa): Fe-15Al: 350(RT), 280(400°C), 250(500°C), 200(600°C), 100(700°C), 50(800°C); Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C: 480, 440, 400, 350, 250, 100; Fe-15Al-2V-1C: 400, 370, 350, 320, 250, 100; Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C: 830, 600, 550, 500, 400, 100; Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C: 860, 680, 620, 550, 450, 100. All show gradual monotonous decrease with temperature; Nb and Ta alloys highest at all temperatures.

Fig. 6 temperature-dependent compressive yield stress for Fe-26Al-based alloys (MPa): Fe-26Al: 650(RT), 250(400°C), 400(500°C), 200(600°C), 100(700°C), 50(800°C); Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C: 450, 300, 350, 300, 250, 100; Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C: 450, 300, 400, 400, 300, 150; Fe-26Al-2V-1C: 550, 300, 350, 350, 250, 100; Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C: 600, 450, 480, 350, 250, 150; Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C: 800, 550, 600, 450, 350, 150; Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C: 600, 380, 450, 350, 250, 150. All Fe-26Al-based alloys exhibit yield stress anomaly with peak around 550°C (~400 MPa for Ti and V). Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C shows peak at 474°C and 619 MPa, highest at all tested temperatures due to Laves phase (Fe,Al)2Nb. MC carbides provide high temperature stability and coarsening resistance compared to Laves phase.

Fig. 7 yield stress at 650°C (compression/tension, MPa): Fe-15Al: 120/80; Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C: 185/130; Fe-15Al-2V-1C: 150/110; Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C: 430/345; Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C: 410/330; Fe-26Al: 125/95; Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C: 250/150; Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C: 300/205; Fe-26Al-2V-1C: 240/130; Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C: 290/195; Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C: 330/210; Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C: 260/155. Asymmetric tension-compression behaviour confirmed at 650°C.

Fig. 8 yield stress at 800°C (compression/tension, MPa): Fe-15Al: 32/24; Fe-15Al-2Ti-1C: 48/30; Fe-15Al-2V-1C: 43/28; Fe-15Al-2Nb-1C: 83/64; Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C: 104/80; Fe-26Al: 35/28; Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C: 90/50; Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C: 108/75; Fe-26Al-2V-1C: 88/45; Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C: 98/60; Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C: 136/82; Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C: 106/62. Asymmetric tension-compression behaviour confirmed at 800°C.

Fig. 4 SEM fracture surface details: Fe-15Al shows very fine transgranular cleavage facets; Fe-15Al-2M-1C alloys show coarse transgranular cleavage; Fe-26Al shows very coarse transgranular fracture; Fe-26Al-2Nb-1C shows very fine facetted transgranular cleavage; Fe-26Al-2V-1C fracture facets form on (001) planes of matrix parallel to (001) planes of VC precipitates; Fe-26Al-2Ti/4Ti/2Ta-1C and Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C show mixed intergranular and transgranular fracture with cube-like precipitates visible on intergranular areas. Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C fractured almost completely by intergranular decohesion. Intergranular areas on Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C are rough due to densely distributed precipitates.
Fe-26Al-based matrix undergoes order-disorder transitions: D03/B2 at ~550°C and B2/A2 at ~800°C before melting. Fe-15Al-based alloys have disordered A2 bcc matrix up to melting temperature.
Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C is three-phase containing NbC and (Fe,Al)2Nb Laves phase in Fe3Al matrix; all other alloys are two-phase Fe-Al matrix with MC carbides.
